zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Burgh於1282年出生在威爾士時，她的父親Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster23歲，母親Margaret Guines23歲。她於1297年1月3日與Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont在英國素福爾可易普斯維其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1322年2月8日享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Eleanor de Burgh于1282年出生在威尔士时，她的父亲Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster23岁，母亲Margaret Guines23岁。她于1297年1月3日与Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont在英国素福爾可易普斯維其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1322年2月8日享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Elinor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor .

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Burgh was born in 1282, in Wales, her father, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, was 23 and her mother, Margaret Guines, was 23. She married Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont on 3 January 1297, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 8 February 1322, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor de Burgh est née en 1282, à Galles, son père, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Margaret Guines, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont le 3 janvier 1297, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 février 1322, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Eleanor de Burgh 1282 in Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster 23 und ihre Mutter Margaret Guines 23. Sie hat am 3. Januar 1297 in Ipswich, Suffolk, England Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Februar 1322 im Alter von 40 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Burgh è nata il nel 1282 nel Galles, sua padre, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Margaret Guines, aveva 23 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont il 3 gennaio 1297, nel Ipswich, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 8 febbraio 1322, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Burghが1282年にウェールズで生まれた時，父Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterは23歳，母Margaret Guinesは23歳であった。1297年1月3日にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチでSir Thomas III de Multon of Egremontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1322年2月8日に40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Burgh(이)가 1282년 웨일스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Guines(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1297년 1월 3일 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1322년 2월 8일 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Eleanor de Burgh nasceu em 1282, em Gales, seu pai, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Guines, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont em 3 de janeiro de 1297, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de fevereiro de 1322, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Burgh родилась в 1282 г. в местности Уэльс, её отцу по имени Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Guines, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont 3 января 1297 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 февраля 1322 г. в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Eleanor de Burgh nació en 1282, en Gales, su papá, Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Margaret Guines, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas III de Multon of Egremont el 3 de enero de 1297, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 8 de febrero de 1322, a la edad de 40 años.
